name: Aloo Gobi

hero.caption: aloo gobi — golden spiced cauliflower and potato

healthFacts: Cauliflower is a crucifer — it contains sulforaphane, studied for its cancer-protective properties.
Turmeric's curcumin is one of the most well-researched anti-inflammatory compounds in food.
Cooking cauliflower with black pepper increases curcumin bioavailability by up to 2000%.
Potatoes are unfairly maligned — they're high in potassium, vitamin C, and resistant starch.
This dish is naturally vegan and gluten-free — no substitutions needed.
Dry-cooking (no added water) concentrates the natural sugars of both vegetables.

utensils.name: Wide heavy pan or kadhai with lid

steps.title: Prep the vegetables
steps.detail: Cut cauliflower into small florets — not too small or they'll turn mushy. Peel and cube potatoes into similar sizes. Pat both dry with a cloth. Uniform size means even cooking.
steps.tip: Dry vegetables = better browning. Moisture is the enemy of a good crust.

steps.title: Temper and aromatics
steps.detail: Heat oil in the pan until shimmering. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 20 seconds. Add sliced onion and cook 5 minutes until translucent. Add grated ginger and garlic, stir 1 minute until fragrant.
steps.tip: Cumin seeds that splutter immediately = the pan is hot enough. Don't add them too early.

steps.title: Add spices
steps.detail: Add turmeric, chili powder, and coriander powder to the onion. Stir for 30 seconds — just long enough to bloom the spices in the oil without burning. The kitchen will smell incredible.
steps.tip: Spices bloom in fat, not water. Adding them to hot oil unlocks the fat-soluble aromatics.

steps.title: Cook the potato first
steps.detail: Add potato cubes and stir to coat in the spiced oil. Cook covered on medium heat for 8 minutes, stirring every 2–3 minutes. Potatoes need a head start since they take longer than cauliflower.
steps.tip: Adding cauliflower too early is the most common mistake — it goes mushy while potato is still raw.

steps.title: Add cauliflower
steps.detail: Add cauliflower florets and chopped tomato. Mix well. Add salt and cover. Cook on medium-low for 8–10 minutes, lifting the lid and stirring every few minutes to prevent sticking.
steps.tip: If the pan looks dry, sprinkle a tablespoon of water on the edges — not in the center.

steps.title: Dry out and finish
steps.detail: Remove lid. Cook on medium-high for 2–3 minutes, stirring often, until any moisture evaporates and you see light browning on the edges. Add garam masala, toss, and scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
steps.tip: This final high-heat step is what gives aloo gobi its characteristic slightly crispy edges.
